title: CIL Electronics & Telecom — Advanced Mock 12 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the currency of the United Kingdom?
questions.solution: The UK's currency is the Pound Sterling (£).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where are the headquarters of the United Nations located?
questions.solution: The UN headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Where is the headquarters of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.solution: The WHO is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is India's national space agency?
questions.solution: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is India's space agency.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Taj Mahal is located in which city?
questions.solution: The Taj Mahal is in Agra, Uttar Pradesh, on the bank of the Yamuna.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Sun Temple at Konark is located in which state?
questions.solution: The Konark Sun Temple is in Odisha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In which year did the Dandi (Salt) March take place?
questions.solution: Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930 during the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Quit India Movement was launched in which year?
questions.solution: The Quit India Movement began in August 1942.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who founded the Maurya Empire?
questions.solution: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Maurya Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many players are there in a cricket team on the field?
questions.solution: A cricket team fields eleven players.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Thomas Cup is associated with which sport?
questions.solution: The Thomas Cup is the premier men's international team badminton event.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bharat Ratna is India's highest:
questions.solution: The Bharat Ratna is the highest civilian award of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution regulates monetary policy in India?
questions.solution: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the country's central bank and monetary authority.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: GST in India was implemented from which date?
questions.solution: The Goods and Services Tax came into force on 1 July 2017.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.solution: Light travels at about 3 × 10^8 metres per second in vacuum.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 160 m long move in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 45 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 160 m, s₁ = 54 km/h, s₂ = 45 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(54+45)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=27.5$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+160}{27.5}$.
**Answer.** $t=10.91$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 3000 for 6 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 3000×6 mo; B: Rs 8000×4 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=18000:32000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{18000}{50000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1080.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 40 L of milk. 10 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 3 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 40 L, x = 10 L, n = 3.
**Working.** $=40\left(1-\tfrac{10}{40}\right)^{3}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 16.88$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 77 km downstream in 7 h and the same 77 km upstream in 11 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 77 km in 7 h; upstream 77 km in 11 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{77}{7}=11$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{77}{11}=7$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{11+7}{2}=9$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:30 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 30.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times30\right|=45^\circ$; smaller $=\min(45,360-45)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=45^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 8 h, but with a leak it takes 10 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 8 h; with leak b = 10 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{8}-\tfrac1{10}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{8\times10}{10-8}$.
**Answer.** $T=40$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 20000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 20000, r = 8% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=20000\left(\tfrac{8}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 128.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 50% above cost, then gives a 25% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 50%, discount d = 25%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{50}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{25}{100}\right)=1.13$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=12.5\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 180 m and 100 m long move in opposite directions at 60 km/h and 36 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 180 m, L₂ = 100 m, s₁ = 60 km/h, s₂ = 36 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(60+36)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=26.67$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{180+100}{26.67}$.
**Answer.** $t=10.5$ s.

questions.stem: If 12 men complete a work in 9 days, 9 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 12×9 = 9×D, D = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 2 : 3 and 6 : 9 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (2×6) : (3×9) = 12 : 27.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 16 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (16+1)/2 = 8.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 14 and 21 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 14 and 21 is 42.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 10 days and B in 20 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 117 is divided in the ratio 4 : 9, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 117 × 9/13 = 81.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 40 and 56 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 40 and 56 is 8.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 12000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 9 gives quotient 14 and remainder 2. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 9×14+2 = 128.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 30 km/h and returns over the same route at 90 km/h. His average speed is:
questions.solution: Average speed = 2uv/(u+v) = 2×30×90/(30+90) = 45 km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 704 incurs a 12% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: No A is B. All B are C. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some C are not A.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 4, 9, 19, 39, 79, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 79 × 2 + 1 = 159.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(5² + 12²) = √169 = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'BID'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of B, I, D to get 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'LAMP' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: LAMP → NCOR.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some cats are dogs. All dogs are animals. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: Some cats are animals.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A is the brother of B. B is the brother of C. C is the father of D. How is A related to D?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Uncle.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 4:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 4:00 the hands are 4 × 30 = 120° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 2, so next = 24 × 2 = 48.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 5, 11, 17, 21?
questions.solution: 5, 11, 17 are prime; 21 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 4, 7, 13, 22, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 34.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 9, 16, 25, 36, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 7² = 49.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 30 people, X is 7th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 30 − 7 + 1 = 24.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, TREE is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get USFF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: DE, GH, JK, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after JK comes MN.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(5² + 12²) = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All pens are tools. All tools are useful. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All pens are useful' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 3 : 9 :: 4 : ? (pattern n : n^2)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Rose, Lily, Lotus, Mango?
questions.solution: Mango belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Hand : Glove :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Glove relates to Hand, Sock relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing south turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from south leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 18 → 9.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equations predict that a time-varying electric field produces a:
questions.solution: Faraday/Ampère–Maxwell coupling of changing E and B fields gives rise to EM waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In amplitude modulation (AM), the quantity varied in proportion to the message is the carrier's:
questions.solution: AM varies the carrier amplitude with the modulating signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The free-space wavelength of a 600 MHz signal is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an AM modulation index μ is correct?
options: μ should be ≤ 1 to avoid over-modulation
μ has no effect on distortion
μ is the carrier frequency ratio
questions.solution: μ > 1 causes envelope distortion (over-modulation); 0 < μ ≤ 1 is required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 16-bit PCM system has a signal-to-quantization-noise ratio of about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LED emits light when it is:
questions.solution: Forward-biased recombination of carriers releases photons in an LED.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about negative feedback is INCORRECT?
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces gain (in exchange for bandwidth, linearity and stability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 10 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Laplace transform is especially useful for analysing:
options: Transient and stability behaviour of systems
questions.solution: The s-domain handles initial conditions, transients and pole locations for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 150 has a base current of 20 µA. Its collector current is about:
questions.solution: Ic = β·Ib = 150×20µA = 3 mA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ideal operational amplifier has:
options: Infinite open-loop gain and input impedance
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: infinite gain, infinite Zin, zero Zout, infinite bandwidth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which gate gives an output of 1 only when both inputs differ?
questions.solution: XOR outputs 1 when the two inputs are unequal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 40 W with a net antenna gain of 10 and a path loss factor of 1000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time-division multiplexing (TDM) shares a channel by allocating:
options: Different time slots to different signals
questions.solution: TDM interleaves samples of several signals in distinct time slots on one channel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A field-effect transistor (FET) is a:
options: Voltage-controlled device with high input impedance
questions.solution: Gate voltage controls the channel current; the gate draws almost no current (high Zin).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A flip-flop is the basic building block of:
questions.solution: Flip-flops store state and form registers, counters and other sequential logic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A summing op-amp (Rf = 20 kΩ) has inputs 2 V via 5 kΩ and 1 V via 10 kΩ. The output is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse-code modulation (PCM) involves sampling, quantizing and:
questions.solution: PCM digitizes an analog signal by sampling, quantizing, then binary-encoding each sample.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM transmitter has modulation index μ = 0.8. The fraction of total power in the sidebands is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A high common-mode rejection ratio (CMRR) in a differential amplifier means it:
options: Strongly rejects signals common to both inputs
questions.solution: CMRR = differential gain/common-mode gain; high CMRR suppresses noise common to both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An antenna is a device that:
options: Converts between guided signals and radiated EM waves
questions.solution: Antennas transduce between currents on a conductor and free-space electromagnetic waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A channel of bandwidth 3 kHz has an SNR of 30 dB. Its Shannon capacity is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-channel enhancement MOSFET enters the saturation (active) region when:
options: V_GS > V_th and V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th
questions.solution: Above threshold and with V_DS pinching off the channel, the MOSFET saturates and acts as a current source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 320 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 320 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 120" width="270" height="120"><line x1="25" y1="90" x2="255" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="95" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M30 30 L150 30 L240 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="104" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f_c</text><text x="34" y="26" font-size="9" fill="#475569">|H|(dB)</text><text x="244" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">log f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Low-pass magnitude response

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NAND and NOR are termed universal gates because they can:
questions.solution: Any logic function can be implemented using only NAND (or only NOR) gates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sampling a signal below the Nyquist rate causes:
options: Aliasing (high frequencies fold to lower ones)
questions.solution: Under-sampling overlaps spectral images, so high frequencies masquerade as lower ones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 10 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:
questions.solution: Nyquist rate = 2·fm = 2×10 = 20 kHz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a sinusoidally modulated AM wave, the maximum fraction of total power in the sidebands (at 100% modulation) is:
questions.solution: At μ = 1, sideband power is Pc·μ²/2 of carrier; total sideband fraction = μ²/(2+μ²) = 1/3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The rectified output waveform shown (alternate gaps between pulses) is produced by a:
questions.solution: Only one half-cycle passes, leaving flat gaps — characteristic of half-wave rectification.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 110" width="250" height="110"><line x1="15" y1="90" x2="235" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 90 Q 45 20 70 90 L120 90 Q 145 20 170 90 L220 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="222" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: half-wave rectified output

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A photodiode is normally operated in:
questions.solution: Reverse-biased photodiodes produce a current proportional to incident light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region of convergence (ROC) of the Laplace transform determines:
options: Whether the system is stable and/or causal
questions.solution: Stability requires the ROC to include the jω-axis; ROC position also encodes causality.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 1 µH and C = 100 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Shannon–Hartley theorem gives channel capacity as:
questions.solution: Capacity rises with bandwidth B and signal-to-noise ratio: C = B log₂(1 + SNR) bits/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two amplifiers of 15 dB and 20 dB are connected with a 3 dB interstage loss. The overall gain is:
questions.solution: Total = 15 + 20 − 3 = 32 dB (dB values add/subtract).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The bandwidth of an AM signal modulated by a message of highest frequency fm is:
questions.solution: AM produces two sidebands, giving a transmission bandwidth of 2fm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an LTI system's impulse response h(t) is correct?
options: h(t) determines only the DC gain
Convolution with h(t) is irrelevant to the output
The system is causal if h(t) = 0 for t < 0
questions.solution: A causal system cannot respond before the input, so h(t) = 0 for t < 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An amplifier has a voltage gain of 31.6. Expressed in decibels this is about:
questions.solution: Gain(dB) = 20·log₁₀(31.6) ≈ 30 dB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bipolar junction transistor (BJT) is fundamentally a:
questions.solution: Collector current is controlled by base current (Ic = βIb) — a current-controlled device.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 150 has a base current of 10 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The wavelength λ of an electromagnetic wave of frequency f in free space is:
questions.solution: λ = c/f, with c the speed of light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Zener diode used as a voltage regulator operates in its:
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener holds a nearly constant voltage, regulating the output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 10 kΩ, Rf = 10 kΩ, Vin = 1 V), the output voltage is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="70" x2="50" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="50" y="62" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="67" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">Rin=10k</text><line x1="84" y1="70" x2="110" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="110,45 110,95 165,70" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="128" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">−</text><text x="128" y="62" font-size="9" fill="#475569">+</text><path d="M97 40 H150 V40" fill="none"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="97" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="115" y="32" width="30" height="14" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="130" y="28" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">Rf=10k</text><line x1="150" y1="40" x2="150" y2="70" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="165" y1="70" x2="230" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vin=1V</text><text x="200" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text></svg>
figure.caption: Inverting op-amp amplifier

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A continuous LTI system is stable if all poles of its transfer function lie in the:
questions.solution: Poles with negative real parts (left half-plane) give decaying, stable responses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two amplifiers of 10 dB and 25 dB are connected with a 6 dB interstage loss. The overall gain is:
questions.solution: Total = 10 + 25 − 6 = 29 dB (dB values add/subtract).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Pentavalent atoms (e.g. phosphorus) donate free electrons, giving n-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an n-bit PCM system, the signal-to-quantization-noise ratio improves by about:
questions.solution: SQNR ≈ 6.02n + 1.76 dB, so each extra bit adds ~6 dB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A non-inverting op-amp has Rf = 10 kΩ and Rin = 10 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In frequency modulation (FM), the carrier parameter varied is its:
questions.solution: FM varies the instantaneous carrier frequency with the message amplitude.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 10 kΩ, Rf = 10 kΩ, Vin = 2 V), the output voltage is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="70" x2="50" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="50" y="62" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="67" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">Rin=10k</text><line x1="84" y1="70" x2="110" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="110,45 110,95 165,70" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="128" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">−</text><text x="128" y="62" font-size="9" fill="#475569">+</text><path d="M97 40 H150 V40" fill="none"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="97" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="115" y="32" width="30" height="14" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="130" y="28" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">Rf=10k</text><line x1="150" y1="40" x2="150" y2="70" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="165" y1="70" x2="230" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vin=2V</text><text x="200" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text></svg>
figure.caption: Inverting op-amp amplifier

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If two amplifier stages of 20 dB and 30 dB are cascaded, the overall gain is:
questions.solution: Gains in dB add: 20 + 30 = 50 dB (i.e. voltage gains multiply).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about diodes is INCORRECT?
options: An ideal diode conducts fully in reverse bias
Reverse leakage current is very small
A Zener operates in reverse breakdown
A diode conducts in forward bias
questions.solution: An ideal diode blocks in reverse bias; it conducts in forward bias.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 40 W with a net antenna gain of 100 and a path loss factor of 1000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine system stability without:
questions.solution: Routh's array tests sign changes in the first column to count right-half-plane roots directly.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 8-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 5 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:
questions.solution: Resolution = FS/2ⁿ = 5/2^8 ≈ 19.53 mV.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ADC is a circuit that converts:
options: An analog signal into a digital code
questions.solution: An analog-to-digital converter samples and quantizes an analog input into digital values.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in a control system primarily improves:
questions.solution: Negative feedback reduces sensitivity to disturbances and improves accuracy and stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 3 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier series can represent any periodic signal as a sum of:
questions.solution: A periodic signal decomposes into a fundamental plus its harmonics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 8-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about a Zener diode regulator is correct?
options: Its voltage rises steeply with current in breakdown
It must be reverse biased below breakdown
It maintains a nearly constant voltage in reverse breakdown
questions.solution: In reverse breakdown the Zener voltage stays almost constant over a range of current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Class C amplifier achieves the highest efficiency but:
options: Conducts for less than half a cycle, producing heavy distortion
Conducts for the full cycle linearly
questions.solution: Class C conducts <180°, so it is efficient but non-linear — used with tuned RF loads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The rectified output waveform shown (every half-cycle is positive) is produced by a:
questions.solution: Both half-cycles appear as positive humps with no gaps — characteristic of full-wave rectification.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 110" width="250" height="110"><line x1="15" y1="90" x2="235" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 90 Q 45 20 70 90 Q 95 20 120 90 Q 145 20 170 90 Q 195 20 220 90" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="222" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: full-wave rectified output

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM is generally preferred over AM for high-fidelity audio because it offers:
questions.solution: FM's constant amplitude makes it far more robust against amplitude noise than AM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 10 V and Amin = 2 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: According to the sampling theorem, a signal of maximum frequency fm must be sampled at a rate of at least:
questions.solution: The Nyquist rate is 2fm; sampling slower causes aliasing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A p-type semiconductor is produced by doping silicon with a:
questions.solution: Trivalent atoms (e.g. boron) create holes, giving p-type material.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 15 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slew rate of an op-amp limits the:
options: Maximum rate of change of the output voltage
questions.solution: Slew rate (V/µs) bounds how fast the output can swing, distorting fast large signals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 1110₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carson's rule estimates FM bandwidth as:
questions.solution: B_FM ≈ 2(Δf + fm), where Δf is peak deviation and fm the highest message frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The region around a PN junction that is depleted of free carriers is the:
questions.solution: Diffusion of carriers leaves a charge-depleted layer with a built-in potential.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The AM waveform's envelope (shown) has Amax = 12 V and Amin = 4 V. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The output of a linear time-invariant (LTI) system is the input convolved with the:
questions.solution: y(t) = x(t) ∗ h(t); the impulse response h(t) fully characterizes an LTI system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 3 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:
questions.solution: Nyquist rate = 2·fm = 2×3 = 6 kHz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A half-adder produces which two outputs?
questions.solution: A half-adder gives Sum = A⊕B and Carry = A·B.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The closed-loop gain of an inverting op-amp amplifier is:
questions.solution: For the inverting configuration, gain = −Rf/Rin.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 0, B = 1, C = 0. The output Y is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="30" x2="60" y2="30" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="55" x2="60" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="34" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">A=0</text><text x="4" y="59" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">B=1</text><path d="M60 18 L85 18 Q105 42 85 66 L60 66 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="74" y="46" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">AND</text><line x1="105" y1="42" x2="140" y2="42" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="140" y2="95" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="99" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">C=0</text><path d="M140 30 Q150 30 165 55 Q150 80 140 80 Q150 55 140 30 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">OR</text><line x1="165" y1="55" x2="220" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="195" y="48" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Y=?</text></svg>
figure.caption: AND–OR logic circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Among amplifier classes, the highest theoretical efficiency but most distortion belongs to:
questions.solution: Class C conducts for <180°, giving high efficiency but heavy distortion (used with tuned loads).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM carrier of amplitude 80 V is modulated by a 30 V message. The modulation index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with a half-wave rectifier, a full-wave rectifier has:
questions.solution: Using both half-cycles, full-wave rectification gives smoother output and better efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for wider bandwidth, lower distortion and better stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LC tank has L = 100 µH and C = 1000 pF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ALU in a CPU performs:
questions.solution: The Arithmetic Logic Unit executes add/subtract and AND/OR/NOT-type operations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC low-pass filter has R = 10 kΩ and C = 10 nF. Its cut-off frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an inverting op-amp circuit, the inverting input behaves as a:
questions.solution: Negative feedback forces the inverting input to ≈ 0 V (virtual ground) when the + input is grounded.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-bit ADC/DAC provides how many distinct output levels?
questions.solution: n bits quantize into 2ⁿ levels; resolution = full-scale/2ⁿ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 14 kHz tone is sampled at 10 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |14 − 10| = 4 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PN-junction diode conducts appreciably only when it is:
questions.solution: Forward bias lowers the barrier and allows current; reverse bias blocks it (except small leakage).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 100 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 100 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 120" width="270" height="120"><line x1="25" y1="90" x2="255" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="95" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M30 30 L150 30 L240 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="104" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f_c</text><text x="34" y="26" font-size="9" fill="#475569">|H|(dB)</text><text x="244" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">log f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Low-pass magnitude response

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectifier circuit is used to convert:
questions.solution: Rectifiers (using diodes) convert alternating voltage into unidirectional (DC) voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Doubling the number of bits in an ADC changes the number of quantization levels from 2ⁿ to:
questions.solution: Levels = 2^bits, so doubling n gives 2^(2n) = (2ⁿ)² levels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 256 distinct quantization levels?
questions.solution: bits = log₂(256) = 8.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A multiplexer (MUX) is a circuit that:
options: Selects one of several inputs onto a single output
Splits one input into many outputs
questions.solution: A MUX routes one of 2ⁿ inputs to the output based on n select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An inverting op-amp has Rf = 100 kΩ and Rin = 2 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The closed-loop gain of a non-inverting op-amp amplifier is:
questions.solution: Non-inverting gain = 1 + Rf/Rin, always ≥ 1 and in phase.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The program counter (PC) in a microprocessor holds the:
questions.solution: The PC points to the memory address of the next instruction to fetch.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The magnitude response shown is flat then rolls off at −20 dB/decade past f_c ≈ 159 Hz. This is the response of a:
questions.solution: Constant gain at low frequencies with a single −20 dB/decade roll-off above f_c ≈ 159 Hz is a first-order low-pass response.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 120" width="270" height="120"><line x1="25" y1="90" x2="255" y2="90" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="95" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M30 30 L150 30 L240 95" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="90" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="104" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f_c</text><text x="34" y="26" font-size="9" fill="#475569">|H|(dB)</text><text x="244" y="104" font-size="9" fill="#475569">log f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Low-pass magnitude response

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A MOSFET is preferred in digital ICs largely because of its:
options: Very high input impedance and low power
questions.solution: The insulated gate gives near-infinite input impedance and low static power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the inverting op-amp shown (Rin = 2 kΩ, Rf = 40 kΩ, Vin = 2 V), the output voltage is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="70" x2="50" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="50" y="62" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="67" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">Rin=2k</text><line x1="84" y1="70" x2="110" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="110,45 110,95 165,70" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="128" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">−</text><text x="128" y="62" font-size="9" fill="#475569">+</text><path d="M97 40 H150 V40" fill="none"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="97" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="97" y1="40" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="115" y="32" width="30" height="14" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="130" y="28" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">Rf=40k</text><line x1="150" y1="40" x2="150" y2="70" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="165" y1="70" x2="230" y2="70" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vin=2V</text><text x="200" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text></svg>
figure.caption: Inverting op-amp amplifier
